name: Herrera and Estepa Loop
name_original: Estepa desde Herrera — circular del Campos de Oliva
description: Tackle the moderate 19.1-mile Herrera and Estepa Loop mountain biking route, featuring challenging climbs and panoramic views.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Herrera offers a diverse landscape characterized by rolling hills, river valleys, and the foothills of nearby mountain ranges like the Sierra de Estepa and Sierra de Gilena. The region features a mix of open terrain and routes that pass by significant water bodies such as the Pantano de Cordobilla and Laguna de Tíscar. This varied topography provides a range of mountain bike trails suitable for different skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Herrera The most popular mountain bike trail is Puente Genil, Jauja, Pantano de Cordobilla – ruta circular, a 33.4 miles (53.8 km) moderate trail that takes about 3 hours 24 minutes to complete, circling a large reservoir. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Laguna de Tíscar and Herrera Loop, a moderate 23.4 miles (37.6 km) path. This route offers views of the Laguna de Tíscar and the surrounding countryside. Local mountain bikers also love the Lora de Estepa desde Estepa — circular por la Sierra de Estepa, a 28.6 miles (46.0 km) trail leading through the Sierra de Estepa, often completed in about 4 hours 17 minutes. Mountain biking around Herrera is defined by routes through mountain ranges, along rivers, and past lagoons and reservoirs. The network offers options for easy mountain bike trails, moderate challenges, and advanced mountain bike routes. The routes in Herrera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 500 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Herrera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Herrera?
A: Herrera offers a wide selection of mountain bike trails, with over 120 routes recorded on komoot. These trails cater to various skill levels, from easy rides to more challenging routes.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Herrera?
A: Yes, Herrera has 18 easy mountain bike trails, perfect for beginners or families looking for a relaxed ride. These routes typically feature gentler terrain and shorter distances. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1270778206/castillo-de-alhonoz-and-rio-genil-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Alhonoz and Río Genil Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate option that can be enjoyed by those with some experience.

Q: What are the typical distances and durations for mountain bike routes in Herrera?
A: Mountain bike routes in Herrera vary significantly. Moderate trails, which make up the majority, often range from 30 to 50 kilometers (18 to 31 miles) and can take anywhere from 2.5 to 4 hours to complete. For instance, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986644555/puente-genil-jauja-pantano-de-cordobilla-ruta-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Genil, Jauja, Pantano de Cordobilla – ruta circular&lt;/a&gt; is 53.8 km and takes about 3 hours 24 minutes.

Q: Are there challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders in Herrera?
A: Yes, for experienced mountain bikers seeking a challenge, Herrera offers 11 difficult routes. These trails often feature significant elevation changes and more technical terrain. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1270711699/lora-de-estepa-desde-estepa-circular-por-la-sierra-de-estepa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lora de Estepa desde Estepa — circular por la Sierra de Estepa&lt;/a&gt;, which is 46 km long with considerable climbing.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on mountain bike trails around Herrera?
A: The region around Herrera offers diverse scenery, including rolling hills, picturesque river valleys, and the foothills of mountain ranges like the Sierra de Estepa and Sierra de Gilena. Many routes also pass by significant water bodies such as the Pantano de Cordobilla and Laguna de Tíscar, providing scenic views.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Herrera?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes in Herrera are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986644555/puente-genil-jauja-pantano-de-cordobilla-ruta-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Genil, Jauja, Pantano de Cordobilla – ruta circular&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1271476891/laguna-de-tiscar-and-herrera-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Tíscar and Herrera Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Herrera?
A: The mountain bike trails in Herrera are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 40 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, scenic landscapes, and the well-maintained nature of the routes.

Q: What are some interesting places to explore near the mountain bike trails in Herrera?
A: While mountain biking, you can explore several historical and cultural sites nearby. Consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5391546" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estepa Castle&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6036466" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcázar and Convent of Santa Clara on San Cristóbal Hill in Estepa&lt;/a&gt;. In Puente Genil, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5356840" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of the Immaculate Conception&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5324875" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Municipal Archaeological Museum&lt;/a&gt; are also points of interest.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by significant water bodies?
A: Yes, several routes in Herrera feature water bodies. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986644555/puente-genil-jauja-pantano-de-cordobilla-ruta-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Genil, Jauja, Pantano de Cordobilla – ruta circular&lt;/a&gt; circles a large reservoir, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1271476891/laguna-de-tiscar-and-herrera-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Tíscar and Herrera Loop&lt;/a&gt; offers views of the Laguna de Tíscar.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Herrera?
A: The best time for mountain biking in Herrera is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) when temperatures are milder and the weather is generally pleasant. Summers can be very hot, and winters can be cooler with occasional rain, though many trails remain rideable year-round.

Q: Can I find mountain bike routes that explore the Sierra de Estepa or Sierra de Gilena?
A: Absolutely. The region's topography includes the foothills of these mountain ranges. You can explore the Sierra de Estepa on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1270711699/lora-de-estepa-desde-estepa-circular-por-la-sierra-de-estepa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lora de Estepa desde Estepa — circular por la Sierra de Estepa&lt;/a&gt;, or ride through the Sierra de Gilena with the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1270642843/estepa-a-aguadulce-circular-por-la-sierra-de-gilena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estepa a Aguadulce — circular por la Sierra de Gilena&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: You'll be glad to have a bit of suspension on these cobbled streets as you climb the hill overlooking the city. You will reach the sites of the Santa Clara Convent and the impressive Alcázar Palace on the hill of San Cristóbal that dominated the city since Moorish times.
Remnants of the rich history and heritage left by the Roman era in Herrara. The Roman thermal baths have been wonderfully preserved. This is a great place to start your adventure.

faqs.question: How long does it take to mountain bike the Herrera and Estepa Loop?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 30.7 km (19.1 miles) long and typically takes around 2 hours and 37 minutes to complete on a mountain bike. This duration can vary based on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Is the Herrera and Estepa Loop suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is rated as moderate and is quite demanding, especially for those unaccustomed to climbs. It requires good fitness and advanced riding skills due to challenging ascents, technical sections, and varied terrain, including water-eroded cuts and stones. It corresponds to an STS S2 technical difficulty level.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied and challenging. You'll encounter a mix of unpaved and paved surfaces, including gravel and ground. Approximately 57% of the route is on tracks and 23% on access roads. Expect technical sections with uneven ground, water-eroded cuts, and stones, particularly on initial sections and during significant climbs.

faqs.question: What can I see along the Herrera and Estepa Loop?
faqs.answer: The route offers views of the Andalusian countryside, including agricultural lands and hills. From higher points, such as the 'Radar' climb, you can expect panoramic views. Notable historical sites along or near the route include the Alcázar and Convent of Santa Clara on San Cristóbal Hill, Estepa and the Roman Baths of Herrera.

faqs.question: Are there any particularly challenging sections on the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, a significant challenge is the 'subida Radar' (Radar climb). This section involves a substantial ascent of 600 meters from Herrera to Estepa, culminating in a 300-meter elevation gain over approximately 2 kilometers to reach the 'Radar'. This climb requires considerable endurance and skill.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride the Herrera and Estepa Loop?
faqs.answer: The best time to ride this loop is typically during spring (March to May) or autumn (September to November). These seasons offer milder temperatures, making the demanding climbs more enjoyable and avoiding the intense heat of the Andalusian summer.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Herrera and Estepa Loop?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas are not detailed, you can generally find parking in the towns of Herrera or Estepa, which serve as common starting points for loops in the area. Look for public parking spaces within these towns near the beginning of the route.

faqs.question: Is the Herrera and Estepa Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: There is no specific information regarding dogs on this particular route. However, when mountain biking with dogs in Spain, it's generally advisable to keep them on a leash, especially near livestock or in populated areas, and always clean up after them.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to access and ride the Herrera and Estepa Loop. It utilizes public tracks and access roads.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects and utilizes sections of several official trails. These include parts of the Estepa Herrera, Cañada Real de Osuna, Camino Pozo del Villar, and Camino Cuesta de los Pescaderos.

faqs.question: What gear should I bring for this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: Given the demanding nature and technical sections, it's recommended to bring a well-maintained mountain bike suitable for varied terrain, a helmet, appropriate cycling attire, plenty of water, snacks, a repair kit (including spare tube/patch kit), and a first-aid kit. Navigation tools like a GPS device or a loaded map on your phone are also advisable.

waypoints.name: Alcázar and Convent of Santa Clara on San Cristóbal Hill, Estepa
